Continuity revalidation FAQ summary

Summary card that should be dropped.

Continuity revalidation FAQ

Continuity revalidation FAQs matter when operators need direct answers about how approval authority should be renewed through prolonged incidents without losing auditability.

The document explains how review checkpoints, queue health evidence, and fallback queues should be refreshed before any revalidation window is extended.

Teams are expected to preserve fairness controls, handoff records, and recovery evidence so revalidation remains traceable across shifts.

Longer incidents also require explicit rollback ownership and a fresh validation window before authority is passed to the next operator.
